Title: MY ShopeePayLater
Content:
Last Update : Dec 30, 2026

MO PIC : Nora Atika

Xendit Stakeholders

Department

PIC

PIC Role

Partnership

Musa Sharuddin

Partnership PIC for Shopeepay

Partner PIC

Name

Email

Dee Lun Kok

deelun.kok@shopee.com

Eligibility

Entity Type

Eligible

Local Domestic MY Entity Sdn Bhd / Bhd

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity Sole Prop

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity Association

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity LLP / Partnership

✅

Foreign Entity

❌

Local / Foreign Individual

❌

Merchant Requirements

Category

Requirements

Prerequisite

SPayLater whitelisting can only be activated AFTER a merchant is onboarded for ShopeePay One-Time or Tokenized payment.

Whitelist Conditions

Ecommerce URL: Merchant must provide a valid URL for an online shop featuring a checkout page and product/service sales.

ShopeePay Tokenized Merchants: SPayLater is restricted to a 1-month tenure only; users cannot select longer tenures for payment.

Prohibited Merchant Categories

● Gambling, gaming or casinos products and services

● Non-halal food and beverages, particularly pork and alcoholic or other intoxicating products such as tobacco, cigarettes, electronic cigarettes etc

● Entertainment including pornography, publications, and music, and the production or distribution or sale of such entertainment

● Gold and Silver

● Religious-related items, idols, statues, worship materials

● Weapon and mass destruction items

● Other immoral and unethical activities

● any other items or business that may pose Shariah and reputational risk to the Islamic SPayLater facility

SLA

Entity Origin

SLA

Local Entity

Whitelisting Period: 2 weeks

Note: This is in addition to the standard SLA required for the initial ShopeePay One-Time or Tokenized payment onboarding.

Onboarding Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

Merchant submits a request via their dashboard by selecting "Yes" for Shopee PayLater when activating ShopeePay One-Time or Tokenized.

Merchant

2

Once a merchant clicks 'Submit' for activation, the request will be reflected in #mo-request.

For SPayLater, please refer to the spreadsheet link; it will state 'YES' in Column Y whenever a merchant requests to activate 'ShopeePay One-Time' and/or 'ShopeePay Tokenized'.

Request and Spreadsheet Example :

Merchant

3

MO activates "ShopeePay One-Time" instantly via Channel Activation Retool

4

MO fills the Spaylater Gsheet Submission for Spaylater request (Columns A-F):

  1. Request Date: Indicate the date of submission. (MM/DD)

  2. Brand Name: Specify the name of the merchant brand.

  3. Merchant ID: Provide the unique Merchant ID

  4. Merchant Store ID: Include the specific Store ID for the merchant.

  5. Merchant Business Categories: Select from the provided drop-down list. Please do not alter or add any new business categories. Should there be a category outside the selection, kindly reach out to us for clarification.

  6. URL: Ensure the link provided is valid and accessible. It should direct to a payment page or display the sale of products. If this is not possible, Shopeepay Partner will not able to whitelist the request

5

MO informs ShopeePay PIC via WAG once the Spaylater Gsheet Submission is updated.

6

ShopeePay PIC processes internal approval and whitelists the merchant.

Activation Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

Two weeks after submitting a request to ShopeePay, MO should check Column P of the spreadsheet for a status update. If there is no update, please follow up via WAG for further information.

Spreadsheet Example :

MO

2

Once status shows “approved,” the MO agent is responsible for notifying the merchant.

3

This is a manual "self-serve" process. A manual email update must be sent to the merchant.

Rejection Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

Two weeks after submitting a request to ShopeePay, MO should check Column P of the spreadsheet for a status update. If there is no update, please follow up via WAG for further information.

Spreadsheet Example :

MO

2

Once status shows “rejected,” MO agent is responsible for notifying the merchant directly of the rejection.

3

This is a manual "self-serve" process. A manual email update must be sent to the merchant.
